title: QIAGEN DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cqs8d","text":"For the isolation of microbial genomic DNA from all soil types, including difficult samples such as compost, sediment, and manure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6rk9t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9bpuk","text":"The DNeasy PowerSoil Pro Kit comprises a novel and proprietary method for isolating microbial genomic DNA from environmental samples. The kit uses QIAGEN’s secondgeneration Inhibitor Removal Technology® (IRT) and is intended for use with environmental samples containing high humic acid content, including difficult soil types such as compost, sediment, and manure. Other more common soil and stool types have also been used successfully with this kit. Improved IRT combined with more efficient bead beating and lysis chemistry yields high-quality DNA that can be used immediately in downstream applications, including PCR, qPCR, and next-generation sequencing (16S and whole genome).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"r291","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ansp8","text":"As of April, 2023 - no published studies successfully detecting fish sedDNA using only this kit (see Lakes ABPS protocol). Unpublished studies report poor DNA yields and low concentrations of fish sedDNA. Other studies targeting migratory fish sedDNA during a spawning run found sufficient fish sedDNA concentrations following this protocol. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"3itb8","text":"Ensure that the PowerBead Pro Tubes rotate freely in the centrifuge without rubbing.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"453a8","text":"If Solution CD3 has precipitated, heat at 60°C until precipitate dissolves.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ea72u","text":"Perform all centrifugation steps at room temperature (15–25°C).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4n9or","text":"Solution EA and Solution C5 are flammable","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4qet8","text":"DO NOT add bleach or acidic solutions directly to the sample preparation waste.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: SPIN the PowerBead Pro Tube briefly to ensure that the beads have settled at the bottom ADD up to 0.25 g of soil sample to the PowerBead Pro Tube ADD 800 µL of Solution CD1 VORTEX briefly to mix
HOMOGENIZE samples thoroughly using one of the following methods:
ADD 200 µL of Solution CD2 VORTEX for 00:00:05
SECURE the PowerBead Pro Tube horizontally on a Vortex Adapter for 1.5–2 ml tubes VORTEX at maximum speed for 00:10:00
USING a PowerLyzer 24 Homogenizer, homogenize the soil at 2000 rpm, °C, for 00:00:00 PAUSE for 00:00:30 HOMOGENIZE again at 2000 rpm, °C, for 00:00:30

USING a TissueLyser II, place the PowerBead Pro Tube into the TissueLyser Adapter FASTEN the adapter into the instrument and shake for 00:05:00 at speed 25 Hz REORIENT the adapter so that the side that was closest to the machine body is now furthest from it SHAKE again for 00:05:00 at a speed of 25 Hz.
CENTRIFUGE the PowerBead Pro Tube at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 TRANSFER supernatant to a clean 2 mL Microcentrifuge Tube (expect 500-600ul)
CENTRIFUGE tubes at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 AVOIDING the pellet, transfer up to 700 µL of supernatant to a clean 2 ml Microcentrifuge Tube
ADD 600 µL of Solution CD3 VORTEX for 00:00:05
LOAD 650 µL of the lysate onto an MB Spin Column CENTRIFUGE at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 DISCARD the liquid flow-through

REPEAT step 7 to ensure that all of the lysate has passed through the MB Spin Column CAREFULLY place the MB Spin Column into a clean 2 mL Collection Tube. Avoid splashing any flow-through onto the MB Spin Column
ADD 500 µL of Solution EA to the MB Spin Column CENTRIFUGE at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 DISCARD the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into the same 2 mL Collection Tube
ADD 500 µL of Solution C5 to the MB Spin Column CENTRIFUGE at 15000 x g, °C, for 00:01:00 DISCARD the flow-through and place the MB Spin Column into a NEW 2 mL Collection Tube
CENTRIFUGE at 16000 x g, °C, for 00:02:00 CAREFULLY place the MB Spin Column into a new 1.5 ml Elution Tube
ADD between 50 µL and 100 µL of Solution C6 to the center of the white filter membrane CENTRIFUGE at 15000 x g for 00:01:00 DISCARD the MB Spin Column DNA is now ready for downstream applications
creator: Yuanyu Cheng
